active_ingredients: Lidocaine 4% topical patch / cream (e.g., for back, neck, shoulder, and joint pain)
indications_and_usage: Uses: temporarily relieves minor pain, soreness, and aching of muscles and joints associated with backache, strains, sprains, bruises, arthritis, and simple muscle soreness. Works as a topical local anesthetic that numbs the area where it is applied.
contraindications: Do not use if allergic to lidocaine or other 'caine' anesthetics. Do not use on damaged or broken skin or over large areas.
warnings_and_cautions: For external use only. Do not apply to wounds, cuts, or irritated/infected skin; do not bandage tightly or apply heat (heating pad) over the patch - this can increase absorption and the risk of serious side effects (irregular heartbeat, seizures, breathing problems). Use only one patch at a time and remove after up to 8-12 hours; do not use more than 3 patches in 24 hours. Wash hands after handling. Stop use if irritation occurs. Ask a doctor before use if you have liver disease.
adverse_reactions: Mild redness, itching, or burning at the application site; rarely systemic effects if overused.
drug_interactions: Generally none at recommended topical doses; use caution if you take other local anesthetics or class I antiarrhythmic drugs.
dosage_and_administration: Adults and children 12 years and over: clean and dry the affected area, apply one patch to intact skin for up to 8-12 hours within a 24-hour period; do not use more than 3 patches at once or in 24 hours; do not reuse a patch. For cream: apply a thin layer up to 3-4 times a day. Children under 12: ask a doctor.
overdosage: Overuse, large areas, or heat can cause lidocaine toxicity: dizziness, drowsiness, confusion, tremor, slow or irregular heartbeat, seizures. Seek emergency care; contact Poison Control (1-800-222-1222).
